mcp.description: Get the current date and time with timezone information.
mcp.name: get_current_time

mcp.description: Read a Google Drive file by its id: returns the file metadata (name, mimeType, size, modifiedTime, webViewLink) plus a best-effort text rendering of its content. Google-native docs/sheets/slides are exported to text; binary files return text only if they decode as UTF-8, otherwise a note explains the content was omitted.

file_id.description: The Drive file id (from search_drive_files or a Drive URL).

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

mcp.name: get_drive_file

mcp.description: Get statistics about the snapshot index - total count, operators, byte ranges, etc.
mcp.name: get_index_stats

mcp.description: Retrieve a previously generated media file (image, video, music) by URL or task_id. Use to verify media exists before using it, or to get metadata about past generations.

task_id.description: Alternative: Task ID from a previous generation (will look up the URL)

url.description: Media URL (e.g., /ui/uploads/sunset_abc123.png from a previous generation)

mcp.name: get_media

mcp.description: Check the status of a music generation task. Alias for get_task_status with music-specific context.

task_id.description: The task ID returned by lyria_music

mcp.name: get_music_status

mcp.description: Retrieve a specific snapshot by ID from the BlackBox memory. Use when you have a snapshot ID from search results and need the full content.
mcp.name: get_snapshot

mcp.description: Check whether an async task is done yet — an image, video, or music generation, a use_computer computer-use run, or a CLI coding-agent task. Answers "is it finished/ready yet", reports progress and whether it is still running, and returns the result URL once complete. This is a one-shot check — call it once when you (or the user) want a status update; you do not need to poll it in a loop, since the live task pill refreshes progress on its own. Takes the task ID the generating tool returned.
mcp.name: get_task_status

mcp.description: List all Gmail labels, or modify labels on a message (mark read/unread, archive, star/unstar).

message_id.description: Required for modify actions (mark_read, mark_unread, archive, star, unstar)

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

mcp.name: gmail_labels

mcp.description: Read the full content of an email by its message ID (from gmail_search results). Returns subject, sender, body text, and labels.

message_id.description: Gmail message ID from gmail_search results

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

mcp.name: gmail_read

mcp.description: Reply to an existing email thread. Automatically addresses the reply to the original sender and prefixes 'Re:' to the subject.

message_id.description: Original message ID to reply to

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

thread_id.description: Thread ID from the original message

mcp.name: gmail_reply

mcp.description: Search or list emails in the operator's Gmail inbox. Uses Gmail search syntax (e.g., 'from:someone subject:hello', 'is:unread', 'newer_than:2d'). Leave query empty for recent emails.

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

query.description: Gmail search query like 'from:someone subject:hello'. Leave empty for recent emails

mcp.name: gmail_search

mcp.description: Compose and send a new email from the operator's Gmail account.

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

mcp.name: gmail_send

mcp.description: Generate images using xAI's Grok image model. Quality-first defaults (16:9). Returns a task_id that completes asynchronously.

numberOfImages.description: Number of images to generate (1-4). Default: 1

prompt.description: Detailed description of the image to generate. Be specific about subjects, style, composition, lighting, colors, and mood.

mcp.name: grok_image

mcp.description: Search the web using xAI Grok's live web search. Returns a synthesized answer with source citations.

query.description: The search query to look up on the web

mcp.name: grok_web_search

mcp.description: Search X (Twitter) in real time using xAI Grok. Returns a synthesized answer with sources from live X/Twitter posts.

query.description: The search query to look up on the web

mcp.name: grok_x_search

mcp.description: List the calendars on the operator's Google account (the calendar list). Returns each calendar's id (use it as calendar_id with the other calendar tools), summary (name), whether it is the primary calendar, and the operator's accessRole.

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

mcp.name: list_calendars

mcp.description: List all devices on the Tailscale mesh network that can be controlled via Computer Use.
mcp.name: list_devices

mcp.description: List events on the operator's Google Calendar within a time window. Provide time_min and time_max as RFC3339 datetimes; recurring events are expanded into individual instances ordered by start time. Returns a list of event objects (id, summary, start, end, status, htmlLink, attendees, ...).

calendar_id.description: Optional. Calendar id to query (default 'primary'). Use list_calendars to discover other calendar ids.

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

time_max.description: Window end (exclusive upper bound) as an RFC3339 datetime, e.g. '2026-06-21T00:00:00Z'.

time_min.description: Window start (inclusive lower bound) as an RFC3339 datetime, e.g. '2026-06-20T00:00:00Z'.

mcp.name: list_events

mcp.description: List all operators (users) that have snapshots in the BlackBox.
mcp.name: list_operators

mcp.description: Get the most recent snapshots. IMPORTANT: Use this at the START of outbound calls to check for context from other models - there may be task details, order information, or instructions provided before this call was initiated.

count.description: Number of recent snapshots to retrieve (default: 3, max: 5)

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

mcp.name: list_recent_snapshots

mcp.description: List available text-to-speech voices from Google Cloud TTS. Returns a large list of voices with language and gender info. When ElevenLabs is configured, ElevenLabs voices (premium and cloned) are also available for text_to_speech via ids prefixed elevenlabs:<voice_id>.
mcp.name: list_tts_voices

mcp.description: Generate 30-second instrumental music using Lyria-002. CRITICAL: Translate user requests into allowed vocabulary. The API REJECTS genre names, style words, and artist references. ONLY USE: instrument names + tempo + texture. Example: 'EDM' -> 'Synthesizer arpeggios with pulsing bass, fast driving tempo'.

prompt.description: TRANSLATED description using ONLY: Instruments (piano, guitar, drums, bass, synth, strings, brass, etc.) + Tempo (fast, slow, moderate, driving, laid-back) + Texture (warm, bright, dark, smooth, aggressive, dense, sparse). FORBIDDEN: genre names, style words, artist names.

mcp.name: lyria_music

mcp.description: Create a new snapshot (memory) in the BlackBox via AI processing. Content is sent through /chat for synthesis, then auto-mint captures the result.

content.description: Content to save - will be processed by AI before minting

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

snapshot_type.description: Type hint (currently ignored - auto-mint determines type)

mcp.name: mint_snapshot

mcp.description: Generate images using OpenAI's gpt-image model. Quality-first defaults (1024x1024, high quality). Returns a task_id that completes asynchronously.

numberOfImages.description: Number of images to generate (1-4). Default: 1

prompt.description: Detailed description of the image to generate. Be specific about subjects, style, composition, lighting, colors, and mood.

quality.description: Rendering quality: 'low', 'medium', or 'high'. Higher = more detail but slower/costlier. Default: 'high'

mcp.name: openai_image

mcp.description: Search the web using OpenAI's web search. Returns a synthesized answer with source citations.

query.description: The search query to look up on the web

mcp.name: openai_web_search

mcp.description: Search the web using Perplexity Sonar AI search. Returns a synthesized answer with source citations.

query.description: The search query to look up on the web

mcp.name: perplexity_web_search

mcp.description: Read a Google Doc by its document_id. Returns the plain text plus a structured list of elements with their startIndex/endIndex (and any object/element IDs) so you can target precise docs_batch_update edits.

document_id.description: The Google Docs document ID (from create_doc or a docs.google.com URL).

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

mcp.name: read_doc

mcp.description: Read a Google Slides presentation by its presentation_id. Returns the deck's title plus a structured list of slides — each slide's objectId and its pageElements (each element's objectId, type, and any text) — so you can discover what to target before editing with slides_batch_update.

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

presentation_id.description: The Google Slides presentation ID (from create_presentation or a docs.google.com/presentation URL).

mcp.name: read_presentation

mcp.description: Read a Google Spreadsheet by its spreadsheet_id. With a range (A1 notation, e.g. "Sheet1!A1:C10") it returns the cell values. Without a range it returns the sheet metadata (each sheet's title + grid rows/cols + sheet_id) so you can discover what ranges exist before reading or editing.

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

range.description: Optional A1-notation range to read (e.g. "Sheet1!A1:C10" or "Data"). If omitted, returns the sheet metadata instead of values.

spreadsheet_id.description: The Google Sheets spreadsheet ID (from create_spreadsheet or a docs.google.com/spreadsheets URL).

mcp.name: read_sheet

mcp.description: Force refresh the snapshot index cache. Use after minting new snapshots.
mcp.name: refresh_index

mcp.description: Roll one or more dice and return the individual results and their sum. Use for random rolls, e.g. 'roll 2 twenty-sided dice'.

count.description: How many dice to roll (1-100). Default 1.

sides.description: Number of sides per die (2-1000). Default 6.

mcp.name: roll_dice

mcp.description: Search the operator's Google Drive for files (any type) using an optional Drive query string. Returns a list of files with their id (use it with get_drive_file / delete_drive_file), name, mimeType, modifiedTime, size, and a shareable webViewLink.

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

page_size.description: Optional. Max number of files to return (default 20).

query.description: Optional. A raw Google Drive `q` search expression. Omit to list recent files. Examples: "name contains 'budget'", "mimeType='application/pdf'", "modifiedTime > '2026-01-01T00:00:00'".

mcp.name: search_drive_files

mcp.description: Your primary memory tool. The BlackBox contains 1,600+ snapshots — an unlimited bucket of memory holding every past conversation, development session, bug fix, decision, user preference, and interaction. Search this FIRST and OFTEN. Everything about the user, their projects, past work, preferences, and recent activity lives here. When in doubt, search.

operator.description: Optional. Omit to include ALL operators on this box; pass a name to scope results to one operator.

query.description: Natural language search query. Be descriptive — 'CSS design token migration' finds more than 'CSS'. Search multiple times with different queries if needed.

mcp.name: search_snapshots

mcp.description: Directly seek and read a snapshot from the volume using byte offsets. Most efficient method for retrieving snapshot content.
mcp.name: seek_snapshot_direct

mcp.description: Apply a raw Google Sheets API batchUpdate to a spreadsheet for full structural editing (add/delete/rename sheets, formatting, merges, conditional formatting, freezing rows, writing cells, etc). You supply the requests array exactly as the Google Sheets API expects it; it is passed through verbatim. Use read_sheet (no range) first to get sheet titles and sheet_id values to target.

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

requests.description: a Google Sheets API batchUpdate `requests` array (full structural editing). Each item is one Sheets request object (e.g. addSheet, repeatCell, updateCells, mergeCells, deleteSheet).

spreadsheet_id.description: The Google Sheets spreadsheet ID to edit.

mcp.name: sheets_batch_update

mcp.description: Apply a raw Google Slides API batchUpdate to a presentation for full structural editing (create/delete slides, insert text boxes and shapes, insert/replace text, add images, format text, move/resize elements, etc). You supply the requests array exactly as the Google Slides API expects it; it is passed through verbatim. Use read_presentation first to get slide and pageElement object_ids to target.

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

presentation_id.description: The Google Slides presentation ID to edit.

requests.description: a Google Slides API batchUpdate `requests` array (full structural editing). Each item is one Slides request object (e.g. createSlide, insertText, createShape, deleteObject, replaceAllText).

mcp.name: slides_batch_update

mcp.description: Transcribe audio to text. provider='elevenlabs' adds speaker diarization (up to 32 speakers), word timestamps, and audio-event tags — ideal for meetings, calls, and interviews. Supports wav, mp3, m4a, ogg, flac, webm.

diarize.description: ElevenLabs only: label who said what (up to 32 speakers). Returns a speaker-attributed transcript.

mint.description: Save the transcript to the BlackBox as a searchable, speaker-attributed snapshot (uses the current operator). Great for meetings and calls you want to recall later.

provider.description: Transcription provider. 'elevenlabs' enables diarization. Omit to use the configured default.

mcp.name: speech_to_text

mcp.description: Kill switch for remote device control: immediately STOPS control_device sessions on the user's phone/tablet/XR device over Tailscale, halting a runaway or unwanted run. Use this the instant the user says 'stop', 'stop controlling my phone', or 'cancel that'. By default it kills ALL in-flight device-control tasks for this operator on the resolved device; pass a task_id to kill just that one. Operator-scoped.

device.description: Optional target device name (tailnet hostname or MagicDNS name). Omit to use origin-aware routing: the device the request came from, else the operator's designated PRIMARY device.

task_id.description: Optional specific device-control task id to kill (as returned in a control_device run). Omit to kill ALL in-flight device-control tasks for this operator on the target device.

mcp.name: stop_device_control

mcp.description: Convert text to speech. Returns an audio URL. OpenAI TTS HD voices: alloy, echo, fable, onyx, nova, shimmer. ElevenLabs voices are also available when configured -- pass the prefixed id as voice (e.g. elevenlabs:<voice_id> for premium or cloned voices); list_tts_voices shows the available ElevenLabs voice ids.

model.description: Model: tts-1 or tts-1-hd (default: tts-1-hd)

text.description: Text to convert to speech

voice.description: Voice: alloy, echo, fable, onyx, nova, shimmer (default: onyx). Or an ElevenLabs voice id prefixed elevenlabs:<voice_id> (premium/cloned voices) when ElevenLabs is configured.

mcp.name: text_to_speech

mcp.description: Your tool discovery system. Use this to find and retrieve tools from the ToolVault. Actions: 'search' finds tools by what you need to do, 'read' gets the full spec for a specific tool, 'list' shows all available tools by category.

action.description: search=find tools by query, read=get full tool spec by name, list=show all tools by category

category.description: For list: filter by category (e.g., 'communication', 'media_generation', 'email'). Omit for all.

query.description: For search: natural language description of what you need (e.g., 'send a text message', 'generate an image')

tool_name.description: For read: exact tool name to retrieve (e.g., 'send_sms', 'generate_image')

mcp.name: toolvault

mcp.description: Update fields of an existing event on the operator's Google Calendar (a partial patch — only the fields you supply are changed). Identify the event by event_id. start/end accept an RFC3339 datetime OR an all-day date, same as create_event. Returns the updated event.

attendees.description: Optional. Replacement list of attendee email addresses.

calendar_id.description: Optional. Calendar id the event lives on (default 'primary').

end.description: Optional. New end. Same format as start.

event_id.description: The event id to update (from list_events).

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

start.description: Optional. New start. RFC3339 datetime (contains 'T') for a timed event, OR an all-day date.

mcp.name: update_event

mcp.description: Write a 2D array of values into a range of a Google Spreadsheet. Values are entered with USER_ENTERED parsing (strings are interpreted as if typed in the UI — formulas, dates and numbers are parsed). Use read_sheet (no range) first to discover sheet titles and dimensions.

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

range.description: A1-notation range to write (e.g. "Sheet1!A1" — the top-left anchor; values fill down/right from there).

spreadsheet_id.description: The Google Sheets spreadsheet ID to write into.

values.description: A 2D array of cell values: an array of rows, each row an array of cell values (strings/numbers/formulas). E.g. [["Name", "Total"], ["Widget", "=B2*1.1"]].

mcp.name: update_sheet_values

mcp.description: Control this computer with an autonomous AI agent that operates the screen like a person. Open a web browser and any website, click buttons, type, fill out and submit forms, log in, scroll and navigate pages; also use desktop apps, run terminal commands, and manage files (full Linux desktop). Use it for online tasks like book a flight, book a hotel, buy something, shop, or complete a form on a website. It runs in the background and returns a task ID; the user can watch it on the live task pill. Check get_task_status only if someone asks whether it's finished yet.

device_id.description: Target device ID from the Tailscale mesh (default: 'blackbox' for local machine). Use list_devices to see available devices.

model.description: Optional. The computer-use model CLASS to run: one of 'opus' (the default), 'sonnet', 'fable', 'gemini', or 'gpt'. 'opus' is the most capable class and is the default; omit this param unless you specifically need a different provider. The backend provider is DERIVED from the class you pick — you do not choose a backend directly. A concrete model id is also accepted.

prompt.description: What to do on the computer (e.g., 'Go to news.ycombinator.com and find the top 3 stories')

url.description: Starting URL to navigate to (optional, defaults to about:blank)

mcp.name: use_computer

mcp.description: Fetch and extract clean content from a specific URL. Use this to read articles, documentation, blog posts, or web pages when you have the URL.

url.description: The complete URL to fetch (must start with http:// or https://)

mcp.name: web_fetch

openai_realtime.description: Run Claude Code as an autonomous coding agent on THIS machine, in the operator's shell with FULL permissions - it can read and write files and run any command in the working directory. Asynchronous: it returns a task ID immediately and runs in the background — the user can watch its progress on the live task pill; check get_task_status if asked whether it's done, and cancel it with the task cancel endpoint. Use it for real multi-step coding and system tasks in a working directory, not for quick questions.
openai_realtime.name: claude_code_task

cwd.description: Optional. Absolute path of a real project directory to run the agent in (e.g. a repo you want it to work on). Omit to run in an isolated per-task scratch workspace created just for this run.

model.description: Optional. The model CLASS to run: one of 'opus', 'sonnet', 'haiku', or 'fable'. Claude Code resolves the class to its newest version for you. Omit to use the CLI's own default model.

prompt.description: The task for the coding agent, written in plain language (e.g. 'add a /health endpoint to app.py and run the test suite').

openai_realtime.description: Run the Codex CLI as an autonomous coding agent on THIS machine, in the operator's shell with FULL permissions - it can read and write files and run any command in the working directory. Asynchronous: it returns a task ID immediately and runs in the background — the user can watch its progress on the live task pill; check get_task_status if asked whether it's done, and cancel it with the task cancel endpoint. Use it for real multi-step coding and system tasks in a working directory, not for quick questions.
openai_realtime.name: codex_cli_task

cwd.description: Optional. Absolute path of a real project directory to run the agent in (e.g. a repo you want it to work on). Omit to run in an isolated per-task scratch workspace created just for this run.

model.description: Optional. A concrete model id passed to the Codex CLI verbatim. This CLI does NOT resolve friendly class aliases, so give an exact model id or omit to use the CLI's own default model.

prompt.description: The task for the coding agent, written in plain language (e.g. 'add a /health endpoint to app.py and run the test suite').

openai_realtime.description: Control an Android device on the Tailscale mesh network using Gemini Computer Use. Can tap, type, swipe, open apps, and navigate the device UI autonomously. Returns a task ID - use get_task_status to check progress.
openai_realtime.name: control_android_device

device_id.description: Device ID from the device registry (e.g., 'brandon-phone'). Use list_devices to see available devices.

prompt.description: What to do on the Android device (e.g., 'Open the Play Store and check StoryBox reviews')

openai_realtime.description: Create a scheduled task (cron job) that runs a prompt on a schedule. Use when the user wants recurring reminders, checks, or automated tasks. Cron jobs run server-side via the /chat path; the interactive/device-bound providers (agents, gemini-agents, realtime, local) are OUT of cron scope and not selectable here.
openai_realtime.name: create_cron_job

delivery.description: How to deliver the result (default: snapshot)

delivery_target.description: Phone number for SMS/voice delivery (E.164 format), not needed for snapshot/notification

frequency_hint.description: Human-readable description of the schedule (e.g., 'Every day at 7 AM')

model.description: A provider word (gemini/openai/claude/grok/custom/computer-use) for that provider's default, OR a specific model id from GET /models/{provider}. Empty = the provider's configured default (Auto). Use 'computer-use' for tasks needing desktop/browser/GUI interaction. 'custom' = user-registered OpenAI-compatible servers from the onboarding wizard.

name.description: A short, descriptive name for this task (e.g., 'Morning Server Check')

one_shot.description: If true, run once and auto-delete (for one-time reminders). Default: false

prompt.description: The prompt/instruction that will be sent to the AI when the job fires

provider.description: Which provider routes this job: one of gemini, openai, claude, grok, custom, computer-use. Optional; if omitted it is derived from the model. 'custom' routes to user-registered OpenAI-compatible servers. The interactive/device-bound providers (agents, gemini-agents, realtime, local) are OUT of cron scope.

schedule.description: Cron expression (e.g., '0 7 * * *' for daily at 7 AM, '*/30 * * * *' for every 30 minutes)

openai_realtime.description: Create a new Google Doc in the operator's Google Drive, optionally seeded with initial text. Returns the document_id (use it with read_doc / docs_batch_update) and a shareable edit URL.
openai_realtime.name: create_doc

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

text.description: Optional initial body text to insert at the start of the document.

openai_realtime.description: Create a new file (any type) in the operator's Google Drive. Supply a name and mimeType; optionally seed it with inline text content (uploaded as media). Returns the new file's id, name, mimeType, and a shareable webViewLink.
openai_realtime.name: create_drive_file

content.description: Optional. Inline text content to upload as the file body (UTF-8). Omit for an empty file or a folder.

mime_type.description: The file's MIME type, e.g. 'text/plain', 'text/csv', 'application/json', or 'application/vnd.google-apps.folder' to create a folder.

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

openai_realtime.description: Create an event on the operator's Google Calendar. Provide a summary (title) and start/end times. Times accept an RFC3339 datetime (e.g. '2026-06-20T09:00:00-07:00' or '...Z') for a timed event, or a plain date (e.g. '2026-06-20') for an all-day event. Returns the created event's id, htmlLink, status, summary, start, and end.
openai_realtime.name: create_event

attendees.description: Optional. List of attendee email addresses to invite.

calendar_id.description: Optional. Target calendar id (default 'primary'). Use list_calendars to discover other calendar ids.

end.description: End time. Same format as start: RFC3339 datetime for a timed event, OR an all-day date (for all-day events this is the EXCLUSIVE end date).

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

start.description: Start time. RFC3339 datetime (contains 'T', e.g. '2026-06-20T09:00:00Z') for a timed event, OR an all-day date (e.g. '2026-06-20').

openai_realtime.description: Create a new Google Slides presentation in the operator's Google Drive. Returns the presentation_id (use it with read_presentation / slides_batch_update) and a shareable edit URL.
openai_realtime.name: create_presentation

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

openai_realtime.description: Create a new Google Spreadsheet in the operator's Google Drive. Returns the spreadsheet_id (use it with read_sheet / update_sheet_values / sheets_batch_update) and a shareable edit URL.
openai_realtime.name: create_spreadsheet

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

openai_realtime.description: Show the past runs of a scheduled task (cron job): when each run fired, whether it succeeded or failed, the error if any, and how long it took. Use to answer questions like 'did the 7am job run?' or 'why did my reminder fail?'.
openai_realtime.name: cron_job_history

job_id.description: The ID of the cron job whose run history to retrieve

limit.description: Maximum number of past runs to return, most recent first (default 20)

openai_realtime.description: Permanently delete a file from the operator's Google Drive by its id. This skips the trash and cannot be undone, so confirm the file_id is correct first.
openai_realtime.name: delete_drive_file

file_id.description: The Drive file id to delete (from search_drive_files).

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

openai_realtime.description: Delete an event from the operator's Google Calendar by its event_id. This cancels the event (and notifies attendees) and cannot be undone, so confirm the event_id is correct first.
openai_realtime.name: delete_event

calendar_id.description: Optional. Calendar id the event lives on (default 'primary').

event_id.description: The event id to delete (from list_events).

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

'stop' is the incident kill switch: immediately halts ALL in-flight device-control on the resolved device for this operator (equivalent to the stop_device_control tool) — use it to abort a runaway or unwanted session. With action='stop' the 'task' is ignored (pass any placeholder).

device.description: Optional target device name (tailnet hostname or MagicDNS name) — targets ANY device on the tailnet. Omit to use origin-aware routing: the device the request came from, else the operator's designated PRIMARY device. Naming a device that is not this operator's origin is never a silent retarget — it must be an explicit target here.

provider.description: Optional frontier brain to drive the device. Omit to use the target device's configured default_provider, else the box default. 'gemini' (validated Android CU), 'claude'/'openai' (cloud vision models, DIY-on-Android), or 'gemma' (the on-device model — the legacy control_phone path). An explicit value overrides the per-device default.

task.description: The natural-language task to perform on the device, e.g. 'open Google Maps and search for coffee near me' or 'what's on my screen right now?'.

function.description: Do a task on the user's OWN phone using the Gemma AI that runs ON the phone itself — the BlackBox wakes it over Tailscale. THIS is the tool for 'do X on my phone', 'use my phone to ...', 'have my phone open/show/check ...'. It drives the phone's on-device Gemma, which is DIFFERENT from control_device (where a cloud model drives the phone instead). Only SAFE actions run remotely. It BLOCKS while the phone wakes Gemma (a cold model load, up to ~75s) — you MUST actually call it and WAIT for its result before you reply.
function.name: control_phone

task.description: The natural-language task to run on the phone, e.g. 'open Google Maps and search for coffee near me' or 'what's on my screen right now?'.

function.description: Create a scheduled task (cron job) that runs a prompt on a schedule. Use when the user wants recurring reminders, checks, or automated tasks. Cron jobs run server-side via the /chat path; the interactive/device-bound providers (agents, gemini-agents, realtime, local) are OUT of cron scope and not selectable here.
function.name: create_cron_job

delivery.description: How to deliver the result (default: snapshot)

delivery_target.description: Phone number for SMS/voice delivery (E.164 format), not needed for snapshot/notification

frequency_hint.description: Human-readable description of the schedule (e.g., 'Every day at 7 AM')

model.description: A provider word (gemini/openai/claude/grok/custom/computer-use) for that provider's default, OR a specific model id from GET /models/{provider}. Empty = the provider's configured default (Auto). Use 'computer-use' for tasks needing desktop/browser/GUI interaction. 'custom' = user-registered OpenAI-compatible servers from the onboarding wizard.

name.description: A short, descriptive name for this task (e.g., 'Morning Server Check')

one_shot.description: If true, run once and auto-delete (for one-time reminders). Default: false

prompt.description: The prompt/instruction that will be sent to the AI when the job fires

provider.description: Which provider routes this job: one of gemini, openai, claude, grok, custom, computer-use. Optional; if omitted it is derived from the model. 'custom' routes to user-registered OpenAI-compatible servers. The interactive/device-bound providers (agents, gemini-agents, realtime, local) are OUT of cron scope.

schedule.description: Cron expression (e.g., '0 7 * * *' for daily at 7 AM, '*/30 * * * *' for every 30 minutes)

start.description: Start time. RFC3339 datetime (contains 'T', e.g. '2026-06-20T09:00:00Z') for a timed event, OR an all-day date (e.g. '2026-06-20').

function.description: Clone a voice from local audio sample file(s) using ElevenLabs Instant Voice Cloning. The cloned voice becomes available in the BlackBox voice selector for text-to-speech. Use when the user wants to create a custom voice from a recording they provide (e.g. an uploaded audio sample). REQUIRES explicit consent: you must first confirm the user owns or has permission to clone the voice, then pass confirm_consent=true.

audio_paths.description: Local file paths to one or more audio samples to clone from (e.g. session-upload paths). More/cleaner samples improve the clone.

confirm_consent.description: Set true ONLY after the user has explicitly confirmed they own or have permission to clone this voice. You MUST ask and get confirmation first — refuse otherwise.

confirm.description: Must be true to actually delete. Confirm with the user first — deletion is permanent.

voice_id.description: The voice id to delete. Accepts the elevenlabs:<id> prefixed form or the raw id.

function.description: Design a brand-new synthetic voice from a text description using ElevenLabs Voice Design (text-to-voice). Two steps: (1) call with just a voice_description to generate 3 candidate preview voices; (2) call again with the chosen generated_voice_id AND a name to save it as a real voice in the BlackBox voice selector. Use when the user wants to invent a voice from a description (e.g. 'a gravelly old wizard') rather than clone a real recording.

name.description: Step 2 only: the display name to save the chosen preview under.

text.description: Optional sample text the previews speak (100-1000 chars). Omit to let ElevenLabs auto-generate the sample text.

voice_description.description: Describe the voice to design (e.g. 'a warm middle-aged British female narrator'). Required for the preview step; reused as the saved voice's description.

function.description: Strip background noise from a recording, isolating the voice. Also useful to clean a noisy sample BEFORE cloning (or just use the clone tool's built-in remove_background_noise).
function.name: elevenlabs_isolate_voice

audio_path.description: Local path to the noisy recording to clean (e.g. a session upload file).

function.description: List the ElevenLabs voices available in this account: the user's own/cloned/designed voices ('My Voices') and the premade/library voices. Use when the user asks what voices they have, which voice to use, or to confirm a clone/design landed in their voice selector.

function.description: Generate full songs UP TO 5 MINUTES with vocals and lyrics using ElevenLabs Music (commercially cleared). Unlike lyria_music there is NO restricted vocabulary - use any genre/style/mood words freely. Params: a natural-language `prompt` + optional `music_length_ms` (3000-300000), `force_instrumental`, OR a structured `composition_plan` (sections with styles + lyric lines) for produced work.

composition_plan.description: Structured plan for produced work: sections with per-section styles and lyric lines. Mutually exclusive with prompt - provide one or the other, not both.

force_instrumental.description: Force a purely instrumental track with no vocals/lyrics (default false).

music_length_ms.description: Desired song length in milliseconds (3000-300000, i.e. 3 seconds to 5 minutes). Optional; the model picks a fitting length when omitted.

prompt.description: Natural-language description of the song - any genre, style, mood, instruments, vocals, or lyric themes (no restricted vocabulary). Mutually exclusive with composition_plan.

function.description: Generate a sound effect (0.1-30s) from a text description using ElevenLabs - e.g. 'rain on a tin roof', 'sci-fi door whoosh'. Set loop=true for seamless looping ambience (rain, engine hum).

duration_seconds.description: Desired length in seconds (0.1-30). Optional - the model picks a fitting length when omitted.

loop.description: Produce a seamlessly looping clip for continuous ambience (rain, engine hum). Default false.

prompt_influence.description: How strictly to follow the prompt vs. add creative variation (0-1). Higher = closer to the prompt. Optional.

text.description: Text description of the sound effect to generate, e.g. 'rain on a tin roof', 'sci-fi door whoosh', 'distant thunderclap'.

prompt.description: The task for the coding agent, written in plain language (e.g. 'add a /health endpoint to app.py and run the test suite').

Returns the deck's title plus a structured list of slides — each slide's objectId and its pageElements (each element's objectId, type, and any text) — so you can discover what to target before editing with slides_batch_update.
function.name: read_presentation

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

presentation_id.description: The Google Slides presentation ID (from create_presentation or a docs.google.com/presentation URL).

function.description: Read a Google Spreadsheet by its spreadsheet_id. With a range (A1 notation, e.g. "Sheet1!A1:C10") it returns the cell values. Without a range it returns the sheet metadata (each sheet's title + grid rows/cols + sheet_id) so you can discover what ranges exist before reading or editing.
function.name: read_sheet

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

range.description: Optional A1-notation range to read (e.g. "Sheet1!A1:C10" or "Data"). If omitted, returns the sheet metadata instead of values.

spreadsheet_id.description: The Google Sheets spreadsheet ID (from create_spreadsheet or a docs.google.com/spreadsheets URL).

function.description: Roll one or more dice and return the individual results and their sum. Use for random rolls, e.g. 'roll 2 twenty-sided dice'.
function.name: roll_dice

count.description: How many dice to roll (1-100). Default 1.

sides.description: Number of sides per die (2-1000). Default 6.

function.description: Save a new contact or update an existing one in the contact book. Use when a user mentions someone new with contact info.
function.name: save_contact

notes.description: Context about who this person is and any relevant details

relationship.description: Relationship to the user (e.g., friend, coworker, doctor)

tags.description: Categorization tags (e.g., family, work, doctor, vip)

function.description: Search the contact book for people by name, phone number, tag, or keyword. Use this before making calls or sending texts to find the person's number.
function.name: search_contacts

query.description: Name, phone number, tag, or any search term to find contacts

function.description: Search and list scheduled tasks (cron jobs). Use when the user asks about their scheduled tasks, reminders, or cron jobs.
function.name: search_cron_jobs

query.description: Optional search query to filter jobs by name or prompt content

function.description: Search the operator's Google Drive for files (any type) using an optional Drive query string. Returns a list of files with their id (use it with get_drive_file / delete_drive_file), name, mimeType, modifiedTime, size, and a shareable webViewLink.
function.name: search_drive_files

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

page_size.description: Optional. Max number of files to return (default 20).

query.description: Optional. A raw Google Drive `q` search expression. Omit to list recent files. Examples: "name contains 'budget'", "mimeType='application/pdf'", "modifiedTime > '2026-01-01T00:00:00'".

function.description: Search for media files by description, prompt, or filename. Use when you need to find a specific image/video/audio that was previously generated or uploaded.
function.name: search_media

media_type.description: Filter by type: 'image', 'video', 'audio', or omit for all types

query.description: Search query - matches against prompts, descriptions, and filenames

function.description: Your primary memory tool. The BlackBox contains 1,600+ snapshots — an unlimited bucket of memory holding every past conversation, development session, bug fix, decision, user preference, and interaction. Search this FIRST and OFTEN. Everything about the user, their projects, past work, preferences, and recent activity lives here. When in doubt, search.
function.name: search_snapshots

operator.description: Optional. Omit to include ALL operators on this box; pass a name to scope results to one operator.

query.description: Natural language search query. Be descriptive — 'CSS design token migration' finds more than 'CSS'. Search multiple times with different queries if needed.

function.description: Send an SMS text message via the TG200 cellular gateway. The message is delivered as a real cellular SMS in a single send (messages longer than 1600 characters are truncated). Use search_contacts first to find the recipient's number.
function.name: send_sms

from_number.description: Which of your own numbers/SIMs to send from. Omit to use the default gateway.

message.description: The text message to send. Plain text only, no markdown. Up to ~1600 characters (longer messages are truncated).

phone_number.description: Phone number in E.164 format (e.g., +15551234567) or 10-digit US format

function.description: Apply a raw Google Sheets API batchUpdate to a spreadsheet for full structural editing (add/delete/rename sheets, formatting, merges, conditional formatting, freezing rows, writing cells, etc). You supply the requests array exactly as the Google Sheets API expects it; it is passed through verbatim. Use read_sheet (no range) first to get sheet titles and sheet_id values to target.
function.name: sheets_batch_update

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

requests.description: a Google Sheets API batchUpdate `requests` array (full structural editing). Each item is one Sheets request object (e.g. addSheet, repeatCell, updateCells, mergeCells, deleteSheet).

spreadsheet_id.description: The Google Sheets spreadsheet ID to edit.

function.description: Apply a raw Google Slides API batchUpdate to a presentation for full structural editing (create/delete slides, insert text boxes and shapes, insert/replace text, add images, format text, move/resize elements, etc). You supply the requests array exactly as the Google Slides API expects it; it is passed through verbatim. Use read_presentation first to get slide and pageElement object_ids to target.
function.name: slides_batch_update

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

presentation_id.description: The Google Slides presentation ID to edit.

requests.description: a Google Slides API batchUpdate `requests` array (full structural editing). Each item is one Slides request object (e.g. createSlide, insertText, createShape, deleteObject, replaceAllText).

function.description: Transcribe audio to text. provider='elevenlabs' adds speaker diarization (up to 32 speakers), word timestamps, and audio-event tags — ideal for meetings, calls, and interviews. Supports wav, mp3, m4a, ogg, flac, webm.
function.name: speech_to_text

diarize.description: ElevenLabs only: label who said what (up to 32 speakers). Returns a speaker-attributed transcript.

mint.description: Save the transcript to the BlackBox as a searchable, speaker-attributed snapshot (uses the current operator). Great for meetings and calls you want to recall later.

provider.description: Transcription provider. 'elevenlabs' enables diarization. Omit to use the configured default.

function.description: Kill switch for remote device control: immediately STOPS control_device sessions on the user's phone/tablet/XR device over Tailscale, halting a runaway or unwanted run. Use this the instant the user says 'stop', 'stop controlling my phone', or 'cancel that'. By default it kills ALL in-flight device-control tasks for this operator on the resolved device; pass a task_id to kill just that one. Operator-scoped.
function.name: stop_device_control

device.description: Optional target device name (tailnet hostname or MagicDNS name). Omit to use origin-aware routing: the device the request came from, else the operator's designated PRIMARY device.

task_id.description: Optional specific device-control task id to kill (as returned in a control_device run). Omit to kill ALL in-flight device-control tasks for this operator on the target device.

function.description: Convert text to speech. Returns an audio URL. OpenAI TTS HD voices: alloy, echo, fable, onyx, nova, shimmer. ElevenLabs voices are also available when configured -- pass the prefixed id as voice (e.g. elevenlabs:<voice_id> for premium or cloned voices); list_tts_voices shows the available ElevenLabs voice ids.
function.name: text_to_speech

model.description: Model: tts-1 or tts-1-hd (default: tts-1-hd)

text.description: Text to convert to speech

voice.description: Voice: alloy, echo, fable, onyx, nova, shimmer (default: onyx). Or an ElevenLabs voice id prefixed elevenlabs:<voice_id> (premium/cloned voices) when ElevenLabs is configured.

function.description: Your tool discovery system. Use this to find and retrieve tools from the ToolVault. Actions: 'search' finds tools by what you need to do, 'read' gets the full spec for a specific tool, 'list' shows all available tools by category.
function.name: toolvault

action.description: search=find tools by query, read=get full tool spec by name, list=show all tools by category

category.description: For list: filter by category (e.g., 'communication', 'media_generation', 'email'). Omit for all.

query.description: For search: natural language description of what you need (e.g., 'send a text message', 'generate an image')

tool_name.description: For read: exact tool name to retrieve (e.g., 'send_sms', 'generate_image')

function.description: Update fields of an existing event on the operator's Google Calendar (a partial patch — only the fields you supply are changed). Identify the event by event_id. start/end accept an RFC3339 datetime OR an all-day date, same as create_event. Returns the updated event.
function.name: update_event

attendees.description: Optional. Replacement list of attendee email addresses.

calendar_id.description: Optional. Calendar id the event lives on (default 'primary').

end.description: Optional. New end. Same format as start.

event_id.description: The event id to update (from list_events).

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

start.description: Optional. New start. RFC3339 datetime (contains 'T') for a timed event, OR an all-day date.

function.description: Write a 2D array of values into a range of a Google Spreadsheet. Values are entered with USER_ENTERED parsing (strings are interpreted as if typed in the UI — formulas, dates and numbers are parsed). Use read_sheet (no range) first to discover sheet titles and dimensions.
function.name: update_sheet_values

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

range.description: A1-notation range to write (e.g. "Sheet1!A1" — the top-left anchor; values fill down/right from there).

spreadsheet_id.description: The Google Sheets spreadsheet ID to write into.

values.description: A 2D array of cell values: an array of rows, each row an array of cell values (strings/numbers/formulas). E.g. [["Name", "Total"], ["Widget", "=B2*1.1"]].

function.description: Control this computer with an autonomous AI agent that operates the screen like a person. Open a web browser and any website, click buttons, type, fill out and submit forms, log in, scroll and navigate pages; also use desktop apps, run terminal commands, and manage files (full Linux desktop). Use it for online tasks like book a flight, book a hotel, buy something, shop, or complete a form on a website. It runs in the background and returns a task ID; the user can watch it on the live task pill. Check get_task_status only if someone asks whether it's finished yet.
function.name: use_computer

device_id.description: Target device ID from the Tailscale mesh (default: 'blackbox' for local machine). Use list_devices to see available devices.

model.description: Optional. The computer-use model CLASS to run: one of 'opus' (the default), 'sonnet', 'fable', 'gemini', or 'gpt'. 'opus' is the most capable class and is the default; omit this param unless you specifically need a different provider. The backend provider is DERIVED from the class you pick — you do not choose a backend directly. A concrete model id is also accepted.

prompt.description: What to do on the computer (e.g., 'Go to news.ycombinator.com and find the top 3 stories')

url.description: Starting URL to navigate to (optional, defaults to about:blank)

function.description: Fetch and extract clean content from a specific URL. Use this to read articles, documentation, blog posts, or web pages when you have the URL.
function.name: web_fetch

url.description: The complete URL to fetch (must start with http:// or https://)
